facts.value: Core v1 focuses on vitals, medications, notes, and anomaly detection.
facts.text: Health dashboard v1 scope: vitals, medications, notes, anomaly detection.

facts.value: FFB_SKU_CANON.md is the source of truth for SKU variants and supplier documents.
facts.text: Supplier scorecard and questionnaire must align to FFB_SKU_CANON.md.

facts.value: JHF risk dashboard should show concentration risk, stop-loss coverage, and portfolio exposure.
facts.text: Jackson Heritage Finance risk dashboard focus is concentration risk, stop-loss coverage, portfolio exposure.

facts.value: An old archived item that should not dominate continuation recall.

episodes.summary: Hermes executor failed because system role format was invalid and empty tool names were passed.
OmniRoute service returned 502 and 521 errors during health checks, blocking progress until the service recovered.
Health dashboard planning session settled on core metrics only for v1 and deferred extra metric types to a later release.

openLoops.title: Review anomaly detection thresholds for the health dashboard
openLoops.summary: Next step is to review anomaly detection thresholds for the health dashboard before release.

openLoops.title: Resolve stale vitals card bug
openLoops.summary: There is still an unresolved dashboard bug causing stale vitals cards on refresh.

openLoops.title: Decide whether medications should support dosage history in v1
openLoops.summary: Pending approval on whether medications should support dosage history in the first release.

openLoops.title: Repair Hermes executor configuration
openLoops.summary: Hermes executor configuration still needs a fix for system role formatting and empty tool names.
